CPSC 100.name: Computational Thinking
CPSC 100.prereq_text: None. Not for students with existing credit for or exemption from CPSC 107, CPSC 110 or APSC 160.
CPSC 100.description: Meaning and impact of computational thinking. Solving problems using computational thinking, testing, debugging. How computers work. No prior computing experience required.

CPSC 103.name: Introduction to Systematic Program Design
CPSC 103.prereq_text: None. Not for credit for students with credit for, exemption from, or concurrently taking CPSC 110 or APSC 160.
CPSC 103.description: Computation as a tool for systematic problem solving in non-computer-science disciplines. Introductory programming skills. No programming experience expected.

CPSC 107.name: Systematic Program Design
CPSC 107.description: Fundamental computation and program structures. Continuing systematic program design from CPSC 103.

CPSC 110.name: Computation, Programs, and Programming
CPSC 110.description: Fundamental program and computation structures. Introductory programming skills. Computation as a tool for information processing, simulation and modelling, and interacting with the world.

CPSC 121.name: Models of Computation
CPSC 121.prereq_text: Pre-calculus 12. Corequisite: One of CPSC 107, CPSC 110.
CPSC 121.description: Physical and mathematical structures of computation. Boolean algebra and combinational logic circuits; proof techniques; functions and sequential circuits; sets and relations; finite state machines; sequential instruction execution.

CPSC 203.name: Programming, Problem Solving, and Algorithms
CPSC 203.prereq_text: One of CPSC 103, CPSC 110, APSC 160, EOSC 211, MATH 210, PHYS 210, COMM 337. Not for students with credit for, or currently enrolled in, CPSC 210 or CPEN 221.
CPSC 203.description: Analysis of increasingly complex algorithmic problems, using a modern programming language and a variety of approaches. Problem decomposition and abstraction guide explorations of topics from applied algorithms, for example Voronoi Diagrams, Markov Chains, Bin Packing, and Graph Search.

CPSC 210.name: Software Construction
CPSC 210.description: Design, development, and analysis of robust software components. Topics such as software design, computational models, data structures, debugging, and testing.

CPSC 213.name: Introduction to Computer Systems
CPSC 213.description: Software architecture, operating systems, and I/O architectures. Relationships between application software, operating systems, and computing hardware; critical sections, deadlock avoidance, and performance; principles and operation of disks and networks.

CPSC 221.name: Basic Algorithms and Data Structures
CPSC 221.prereq_text: One of CPSC 210, CPEN 221 and either (a) one of CPSC 121, MATH 220 or (b) a score of 68% or higher in MATH 226.
CPSC 221.description: Design and analysis of basic algorithms and data structures; algorithm analysis methods, searching and sorting algorithms, basic data structures, graphs and concurrency.

CPSC 259.name: Data Structures and Algorithms for Electrical Engineers
CPSC 259.description: Advanced procedural programming. Fundamental algorithms for sorting and searching. Data structures including lists, trees, and hash tables. Introduction to scripting languages and file input/output.

CPSC 261.name: Basics of Computer Systems
CPSC 261.prereq_text: One of EECE 259, CPEN 211 and one of EECE 210, CPEN 221.
CPSC 261.description: Software architecture, operating systems, and I/O architectures. Relationships between application software, operating systems, and computing hardware; critical sections, deadlock avoidance, and performance; principles and operation of disks and networks.

CPSC 302.name: Numerical Computation for Algebraic Problems
CPSC 302.prereq_text: One of CPSC 103, CPSC 110, CPEN 221, EOSC 211, PHYS 210 and one of MATH 101, MATH 103, MATH 105, MATH 121, SCIE 001 and one of MATH 152, MATH 221, MATH 223.
CPSC 302.description: Numerical techniques for basic mathematical processes involving no discretization, and their analysis. Solution of linear systems, including analysis of round-off errors; norms and condition number; introduction to iterative techniques in linear algebra, including eigenvalue problems; solution to nonlinear equations.

CPSC 303.name: Numerical Approximation and Discretization
CPSC 303.prereq_text: One of CPSC 103, CPSC 110, CPEN 221, EOSC 211, PHYS 210 and one of MATH 101, MATH 103, MATH 105, MATH 121, SCIE 001 and one of MATH 152, MATH 221, MATH 223.
CPSC 303.description: Numerical techniques for basic mathematical processes involving discretization, and their analysis. Interpolation and approximation, including splines and least squares data fitting; numerical differentiation and integration; introduction to numerical initial value ordinary differential equations.

CPSC 304.name: Introduction to Relational Databases
CPSC 304.description: Overview of database systems, ER models, logical database design and normalization, formal relational query languages, SQL and other commercial languages, data warehouses, special topics.

CPSC 310.name: Introduction to Software Engineering
CPSC 310.description: Specification, design, validation, evolution and construction of modern software systems, within the context of socially and professionally relevant domains such as ethics, intellectual property, and information security.

CPSC 311.name: Definition of Programming Languages
CPSC 311.description: Comparative study of advanced programming language features. Statement types, data types, variable binding, parameter passing mechanisms. Methods for syntactic and semantic description of programming languages.

CPSC 312.name: Functional and Logic Programming
CPSC 312.description: Principles of symbolic computing, using languages based upon first-order logic and the lambda calculus. Algorithms for implementing such languages. Applications to artificial intelligence and knowledge representation.

CPSC 313.name: Computer Hardware and Operating Systems
CPSC 313.prereq_text: CPSC 213 and either CPSC 221 or DSCI 221.
CPSC 313.description: Instruction sets, pipelining, code optimization, caching, virtual memory management, dynamically linked libraries, exception processing, execution time of programs.

CPSC 314.name: Computer Graphics
CPSC 314.prereq_text: CPSC 221 and one of MATH 200, MATH 217, MATH 226, MATH 253 and one of MATH 152, MATH 221, MATH 223.
CPSC 314.description: Human vision and colour; geometric transformations; algorithms for 2-D and 3-D graphics; hardware and system architectures; shading and lighting; animation.

CPSC 317.name: Introduction to Computer Networking
CPSC 317.prereq_text: CPSC 213 and either CPSC 221 or DSCI 221.
CPSC 317.description: Computer networking, basic communication protocols, network infrastructure and routing. Common application-level protocols and principles associated with developing distributed applications.

CPSC 319.name: Software Engineering Project
CPSC 319.description: The design, implementation, and test of a large software system, using a team approach.

CPSC 320.name: Intermediate Algorithm Design and Analysis
CPSC 320.prereq_text: All of (a) CPSC 221 or DSCI 221, (b) at least 3 credits from MATH or STAT at 200 level or above.
CPSC 320.description: Systematic study of basic concepts and techniques in the design and analysis of algorithms, illustrated from various problem areas. Topics include models of computation; choice of data structures; and graph-theoretic, algebraic, and text processing algorithms.

CPSC 322.name: Introduction to Artificial Intelligence
CPSC 322.description: Problem-solving and planning. State/action models and graph searching. Natural language understanding. Computational vision. Applications of artificial intelligence.

CPSC 330.name: Applied Machine Learning
CPSC 330.prereq_text: Either (a) one of CPSC 203, CPSC 210, CPEN 221, DSCI 221 or (b) MATH 210 and one of CPSC 107, CPSC 110.
CPSC 330.description: Application of machine learning tools, with an emphasis on solving practical problems. Data cleaning, feature extraction, supervised and unsupervised machine learning, reproducible workflows, and communicating results.

CPSC 340.name: Machine Learning and Data Mining
CPSC 340.prereq_text: All of (a) CPSC 221 or DSCI 221, (b) one of MATH 152, MATH 221, MATH 223, (c) one of MATH 200, MATH 217, MATH 226, MATH 253, MATH 254, (d) one of STAT 241, STAT 251, ECON 325, ECON 327, MATH 302, STAT 302, MATH 318.
CPSC 340.description: Models of algorithms for dimensionality reduction, nonlinear regression, classification, clustering and unsupervised learning; applications to computer graphics, computer games, bio-informatics, information retrieval, e-commerce, databases, computer vision and artificial intelligence.

CPSC 344.name: Introduction to Human Computer Interaction Methods
CPSC 344.description: Basic tools and techniques, teaching a systematic approach to interface design, task analysis, analytic and empirical evaluation methods.

CPSC 368.name: Databases in Data Science
CPSC 368.prereq_text: One of CPSC 203, CPSC 210, CPEN 221, DSCI 221.
CPSC 368.description: Overview of relational and non-relational database systems, role and usage of a database when querying data, data modelling, query languages, and query optimization.

CPSC 402.name: Numerical Linear Algebra
CPSC 402.description: Investigation of the practical techniques of computational linear algebra. Orthogonal transformations and their application to the solution of linear equations, the eigenproblem, and linear least squares. Complete solution of the symmetric eigenproblem, including bisection and the QR method. Refinements of these techniques for sparse matrices.

CPSC 404.name: Advanced Relational Databases
CPSC 404.prereq_text: CPSC 304 and one of CPSC 213, CPSC 261, CPEN 212.
CPSC 404.description: Physical database design, indexing, external mergesort, relational query processing and optimization, transaction processing, concurrency control, crash recovery, special topics.

CPSC 406.name: Computational Optimization
CPSC 406.description: Formulation and analysis of algorithms for continuous and discrete optimization problems; linear, nonlinear, network, dynamic, and integer optimization; large-scale problems; software packages and their implementation; duality theory and sensitivity.

CPSC 410.name: Advanced Software Engineering
CPSC 410.prereq_text: Either (a) CPSC 310 or (b) all of CPEN 321, CPEN 331.
CPSC 410.description: Specification, design, construction and validation of multi-version software systems.

CPSC 411.name: Introduction to Compiler Construction
CPSC 411.description: A practical introduction to lexical analysis, syntactic analysis, type-checking, code generation and optimization. This will be used to design and implement a compiler for a small language.

CPSC 415.name: Operating Systems Design and Implementation
CPSC 415.prereq_text: All of the following with a minimum grade of 76% in each: CPSC 310 or CPEN 321; CPSC 313 or CPEN 331; CPSC 317 or ELEC 331.
CPSC 415.description: Team-based design and implementation of a complete operating system, with emphasis on applicability to modern hardware and the practical implementation of operating systems concepts.

CPSC 416.name: Distributed Systems
CPSC 416.prereq_text: One of CPSC 313, CPEN 331 and one of CPSC 317, ELEC 331.
CPSC 416.description: Concepts and design of distributed systems. Communication architecture and models for interprocess communication. Process migration, naming, distributed file systems, fault tolerance, and concurrency control.

CPSC 417.name: Computer Networking
CPSC 417.description: Network protocols and architecture including internetworking, the Internet, layered communication protocols, routing, flow and congestion control, network performance, wired and wireless data communication.

CPSC 418.name: Parallel Computation
CPSC 418.prereq_text: CPSC 320 and one of CPSC 261, CPSC 313, CPEN 212, CPEN 411.
CPSC 418.description: Algorithms, architectures, and programming paradigms for parallel computation. Shared memory, message passing, and data parallel architectures and programming models. Parallel algorithms including reduce, scan, and sorting networks. Reasoning about the correctness of parallel programs. Performance analysis and measurement for parallel programs.

CPSC 420.name: Advanced Algorithms Design and Analysis
CPSC 420.description: The study of advanced topics in the design and analysis of algorithms and associated data structures. Topics include algorithms for graph-theoretic; algebraic and geometric problems; algorithms on nonsequential models; complexity issues; approximation algorithms.

CPSC 421.name: Introduction to Theory of Computing
CPSC 421.prereq_text: One of CPSC 320 or MATH 220 or MATH 226.
CPSC 421.description: Characterizations of computability (using machines, languages and functions). Universality, equivalence and Church's thesis. Unsolvable problems. Restricted models of computation. Finite automata, grammars and formal languages.

CPSC 422.name: Intelligent Systems
CPSC 422.description: Principles and techniques underlying the design, implementation and evaluation of intelligent computational systems. Applications of artificial intelligence to natural language understanding, image understanding and computer-based expert and advisor systems. Advanced symbolic programming methodology.

CPSC 424.name: Geometric Modelling
CPSC 424.prereq_text: One of MATH 152, MATH 221, MATH 223 and one of MATH 200, MATH 217, MATH 226, MATH 253.
CPSC 424.description: Digital representation of curves and surfaces, including splines, subdivision surfaces and meshes. Principles, algorithms and mathematical foundations for geometry representation in computer graphics, computer vision, fabrication, CAD/CAM, and medical imaging. Algorithms for acquisition, creation, representation, and processing of 3D shapes.

CPSC 425.name: Computer Vision
CPSC 425.prereq_text: CPSC 221 and one of MATH 200, MATH 217, MATH 226, MATH 253, MATH 254 and one of MATH 111, MATH 131, MATH 152, MATH 221, MATH 223.
CPSC 425.description: Introduction to the processing and interpretation of images. Image sensing, sampling, and filtering. Algorithms for colour analysis, texture description, stereo imaging, motion interpretation, 3D shape recovery, and recognition.

CPSC 426.name: Computer Animation
CPSC 426.description: Motion in computer graphics for characters and their environments. Keyframing, inverse kinematics, particle systems, rigid body dynamics, contact and collision, controller-based active motion, motion capture.

CPSC 427.name: Video Game Programming
CPSC 427.prereq_text: CPSC 314 and one of MATH 200, MATH 217, MATH 226, MATH 253 and one of MATH 152, MATH 221, MATH 223.
CPSC 427.description: Video game programming techniques and technologies, including rendering, animation, interaction, game AI, real-time software development for games; start-to-finish simple video-game design and implementation.

CPSC 430.name: Computers and Society
CPSC 430.prereq_text: 3 credits of Computer Science and at least third-year standing.
CPSC 430.description: Impact of computer technology on society; historical perspectives; social and economic consequences of large-scale information processing systems and automatic control; legal and ethical problems in computer applications. Computers and the individual: machine versus human capabilities, fact and fancy; problematic interface between man and machine.

CPSC 440.name: Advanced Machine Learning
CPSC 440.description: Advanced machine learning techniques focusing on probabilistic models. Deep learning and differentiable programming, exponential families and Bayesian inference, probabilistic graphical models and other generative models, Monte Carlo and variational inference methods.

CPSC 444.name: Advanced Methods for Human Computer Interaction
CPSC 444.prereq_text: CPSC 344 and one of STAT 200, STAT 201, STAT 203, STAT 241, STAT 251, BIOL 300, COMM 291, ECON 325, ECON 327, FRST 231, GEOG 374, KIN 206, KIN 371, POLI 380, PSYC 218, PSYC 278, PSYC 366, SOCI 328.
CPSC 444.description: Design and evaluation methodologies and theories; formal models of the user including visual, motor, and information processing; advanced evaluation methods including laboratory experiments and field studies; HCI research frontiers.

CPSC 445.name: Algorithms in Bioinformatics
CPSC 445.prereq_text: CPSC 320 and either BMEG 250 or six credits of BIOL beyond BIOL 111.
CPSC 445.description: Sequence alignment, phylogenetic tree reconstruction, prediction of RNA and protein structure, gene finding and sequence annotation, gene expression, and biomolecular computing.

CPSC 447.name: Introduction to Visualization
CPSC 447.description: Design and implementation of static and interactive visualizations. Selection of appropriate visualization methods for given combinations of data type and intended analysis task. Assessment of visual representations according to design and perceptual principles.

CPSC 455.name: Applied Industry Practices
CPSC 455.description: Hands-on project, mentored by industry experts, integrating skills relevant to early career in the computing industry: technical skills, communication, teamwork, networking and portfolio building.

DSCI 100.name: Introduction to Data Science
DSCI 100.description: Use of data science tools to summarize, visualize, and analyze data. Sensible workflows and clear interpretations are emphasized.

DSCI 200.name: Navigating Data: Acquisition, Exploration and Management
DSCI 200.description: Acquiring data through web scraping and generating synthetic data via simulations. Sampling and experimental designs. Reporting and visualizing data and data quality. Data privacy and ownership. Emphasis on case studies and real data sets.

DSCI 220.name: Discrete Mathematics for Data Science
DSCI 220.prereq_text: All of (a) DSCI 100; (b) MATH 100, 110, 120, 180 or SCIE 001; (c) CPSC 103 or CPSC 110.
DSCI 220.description: Boolean algebra and logical inference, proof techniques, sets, relations and functions, regular expressions, and data filtering.

DSCI 221.name: Data Structures for Data Science
DSCI 221.prereq_text: (a) DSCI 220, or (b) CPSC 121 and one of CPSC 203, CPSC 210, CPEN 221.
DSCI 221.description: Object-oriented programming. Design and analysis of basic algorithms and data structures especially relevant to data science. Algorithm analysis methods, basic data structures, graphs and graph algorithms.

DSCI 310.name: Reproducible and Trustworthy Workflows for Data Science
DSCI 310.prereq_text: One of (a) DSCI 221, (b) DSCI 100 and one of CPSC 203, CPSC 210, CPEN 221, or (c) DSCI 100, MATH 210 or ECON 323, and CPSC 107 or CPSC 110.
DSCI 310.description: Data science methods to automate the running and testing of code and analytic reports, manage data analysis software dependencies, package and deploy software for data analysis, and collaborate with others using version control.

DSCI 320.name: Visualization for Data Science
DSCI 320.prereq_text: STAT 201 and one of CPSC 203, CPSC 210, CPEN 221, DSCI 221.
DSCI 320.description: Analysis, design, and implementation of static and interactive visual representations; visualization literacy; data communication; exploratory Data Analysis; application of theoretical principles to visualization development.

DSCI 430.name: Fairness, Accountability, Transparency and Ethics (FATE) in Data Science
DSCI 430.prereq_text: One of CPSC 330, CPSC 340, STAT 301, STAT 406.
DSCI 430.description: Ethical application of data science and machine learning algorithms. Application of ethical theories in real-world case studies. Data ownership, collection, and validity. Algorithm auditing, fairness and transparency. Reducing unfairness in algorithms. Deployment of predictive models and dissemination of results.

MATH 100.name: Differential Calculus with Applications to Physical Sciences and Engineering
MATH 100.prereq_text: Pre-calculus 12 with a score of 67% or higher, or MATH 12 with a score of 80% or higher.
MATH 100.description: Derivatives of elementary functions. Applications and modelling: graphing, optimization. Calculus I.

MATH 101.name: Integral Calculus with Applications to Physical Sciences and Engineering
MATH 101.prereq_text: One of MATH 100, MATH 102, MATH 104, MATH 110, MATH 120, MATH 180, MATH 184, SCIE 001.
MATH 101.description: The definite integral, integration techniques, applications, modelling, infinite series. Calculus II.

MATH 152.name: Linear Systems
MATH 152.description: Systems of linear equations, operations on matrices, determinants, eigenvalues and eigenvectors, vector geometry, complex numbers, applications.

MATH 200.name: Calculus III - Several Variable Calculus
MATH 200.prereq_text: One of MATH 101, MATH 103, MATH 105, MATH 121, SCIE 001.
MATH 200.description: Multivariable calculus (Calculus III). Partial derivatives, the chain rule, extrema, Lagrange multipliers, multiple integrals with applications.

MATH 220.name: Mathematical Proof
MATH 220.prereq_text: One of MATH 101, MATH 103, MATH 105, MATH 121, SCIE 001.
MATH 220.description: Sets and functions; proofs by induction and contradiction; real numbers; supremum and infimum; limits and continuity. Topics may include uniform continuity, the topology of the real line, the Riemann integral, sequences and series of functions.

MATH 221.name: Matrix Algebra
MATH 221.prereq_text: One of MATH 101, MATH 103, MATH 105, MATH 121, SCIE 001.
MATH 221.description: Systems of linear equations, matrix algebra, determinants, vector spaces, dimension, inner product spaces, linear transformations, eigenvalues, diagonalization.

STAT 200.name: Elementary Statistics for Applications
STAT 200.prereq_text: One of MATH 100, MATH 102, MATH 104, MATH 110, MATH 120, MATH 180, MATH 184.
STAT 200.description: Sampling, descriptive statistics, point and interval estimation, hypothesis testing, analysis of variance, contingency tables, regression.

STAT 251.name: Elementary Statistics
STAT 251.prereq_text: One of MATH 101, MATH 103, MATH 105, MATH 121, SCIE 001.
STAT 251.description: Probability theory, random variables and probability distributions, expectation and variance, sampling distributions, point estimation, interval estimation, hypothesis testing, regression.

STAT 302.name: Introduction to Probability
STAT 302.prereq_text: One of MATH 200, MATH 217, MATH 226, MATH 253.
STAT 302.description: Basic notions of probability, random variables, expectation and conditional expectation, limit theorems.

STAT 306.name: Finding Relationships in Data
STAT 306.prereq_text: One of STAT 200, STAT 241, STAT 251, BIOL 300.
STAT 306.description: Theory and application of linear regression: least squares estimation, the Gauss-Markov theorem, hypothesis testing and confidence intervals, prediction intervals, model selection, regression diagnostics, weighted least squares, transformations, polynomial regression.
